name: Tetracycline sensor (TetR + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: Detects tetracycline antibiotic contamination in water and food. The classic TetR sensor gates a CRISPRi circuit driving a visible pigment, in safe E. coli K-12.

input.detects: Tetracycline-class antibiotic residues in water, food, and wastewater
input.operatingRange: TetR responds from low nM aTc

sensing.sensorMechanism: TetR de-represses the Ptet promoter on binding tetracycline/aTc.
sensing.signalFlow: Tetracycline -> TetR releases Ptet -> transcribes an anti-pigment sgRNA -> CRISPRi represses a constitutive amilCP cassette -> pigment fades with antibiotic (NOT). Pair an inverter for colour-on.

parts.name: TetR regulator
parts.source: Tn10 tet operon (also a Marionette/standard sensor)
parts.notes: Tetracycline/aTc-responsive repressor; one of the best-characterised regulators.

performance.notes: TetR/Ptet is among the most characterised regulator-promoter pairs in biology; integrated CRISPRi device is a design template.

safety.biocontainment: Lab/contained; add kill-switch for field use.
safety.notes: E. coli K-12 non-pathogenic BSL-1 host; relevant to antibiotic-pollution surveillance. Note: do not select the host with tetracycline resistance.

buildSteps.title: Design anti-pigment sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target the amilCP promoter; check host off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: TetR + Ptet -> sgRNA. TU2: dCas9 + constitutive amilCP. Avoid TetR-resistance markers.

buildSteps.title: Transform E. coli K-12
buildSteps.description: Select (non-tet marker); confirm baseline pigment without antibiotic.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Add water/food sample + tetracycline standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the classic TetR tetracycline sensor with E. coli CRISPRi
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
